newsSince: No material new company-specific news since June 9. The DC-driven story (Advnsys FY25 revenue EUR1.66B +12%, trading profit +17%, backlog +24%, 2026 order intake running ~2x prior year, ~EUR300m EBITDA target for 2026) and the January decision to scrap the Advnsys IPO/keep it wholly owned both predate the window. H1 2026 results are the next catalyst.

sources.label: Bloomberg: Kingspan record revenue on DC boom
Irish Times: Kingspan axes Advnsys spin-out

verdicts.rankRationale: Latent AI-node lens: Tate (raised floor/containment/DC airflow) is a genuinely dominant, under-screened hyperscale white-space franchise with orders running ~2x y/y. No new June news, but the structural setup remains intact and well-positioned. Hold rank 21 with a slight positive bias pending H1.
verdicts.bull: Tate/Advnsys is the de facto hyperscale raised-floor and containment standard; orders ~2x y/y, ~EUR300m EBITDA in 2026, target to double EBITDA in 4-5yrs, still priced as a construction cyclical.
verdicts.neutral: DC unit is small vs group; the rest is European insulated-panel cyclical that masks the AI-infra optionality.
verdicts.bear: Group remains macro-construction levered; the DC narrative may already be partly priced after the FY25 pop and IPO-scrap commentary.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: H1 2026 results confirming doubled Advnsys order intake and growing standalone disclosure forcing a SOTP re-rate.
verdicts.primaryRisk: European construction downturn drags the consolidated multiple; hyperscale capex air-pocket.
